mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in retail sales signals improving household demand and near-term revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the sign of 3-month RSAFS momentum has no positive relation to 20-day S&P 500 returns, with Rank IC below 0.03 over two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A high and rising federal funds rate raises equity discount rates and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5% and its 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if mean triggered returns exceed unconditional mean returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in unemployment signals deteriorating labor income and rising recession risk, reducing expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns after UNRATE increases above the trigger are not lower than baseline returns, or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth during restrictive monetary policy keeps wage pressure and policy tightness elevated, so discount-rate drag dominates earnings support and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is above 4.0% and PAYEMS 3-month growth is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-FEDFUNDS trigger has no incremental predictive power versus either series alone, or if triggered 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals improving real activity and operating leverage for cyclical firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change crosses from negative to positive
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO momentum crossovers from negative to positive fail to deliver higher 60-day returns than non-triggered periods at the 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and future durable-goods demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 10% over 6 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day returns following the HOUST decline trigger are not below unconditional 60-day returns, or if the downside hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices while policy rates remain elevated preserves restrictive monetary conditions and reduces equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth rises and FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS interaction coefficient is not negative in walk-forward tests or if triggered 20-day returns exceed unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production signals an immediate negative growth shock and inventory adjustment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day returns after the INDPRO contraction trigger are not lower than unconditional 5-day returns, or if Rank IC is negative in fewer than 55% of rolling annual windows.
